old_claim.note: Renewable Energy Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Grid-scale batteries serve load leveling, peak shaving, regulation, and reserve functions independently of renewable generation; renewable integration is an application, not a prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: U.S. Congress Office of Technology Assessment, Chapter 9, report pp. 280-281 (PDF pp. 29-30): BEST began operating in 1982, and utility battery uses listed include load leveling, peak shaving, regulation, and spinning reserve without a renewable-generation requirement.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Megawatt-scale grid batteries were developed and tested for general utility services before modern high-renewable grid applications.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Renewable generation can create demand for storage, but it is neither the origin nor a constitutive input of grid-scale battery systems.

ontology_before: Grid-scale battery storage depended directly on the later renewable-grid-integration node, reversing a relationship in which storage often supports renewable integration.
ontology_after: Grid batteries depend on their battery lineage and an electrical grid; renewable integration remains a downstream use case rather than an upstream prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-grid-battery-green-hydrogen-boundary: renewable_energy_systems is absent from grid_scale_battery_storage.
would_reject_if: A source shows the scoped 1982 grid-battery milestone could not operate or provide utility services without renewable generation.
short_reason: Utility-scale batteries predate high-renewable grids and provide multiple non-renewable grid services.
